title: Brian Bieza
text: Brian Bieza is a Graduate of California State University, Monterey Bay with a degree in Marine Science, as well as a certified AAUS science diver.

title: Brian Bieza
text:
Education
California State University Monterey Bay | Monterey Bay, CA
B.S. in Marine Science | 2024 -2026
Santa Barbara City College | Santa Barbara, CA AA in Biological Science |2015 - 2023

title: About
text: Hello, My name is Brian Bieza, I’m from Goleta, California and acquired my Bachelors degree at California State University Monterey Bay. Living along the coastline has fostered a love for the ocean and at 16 I acquired my open water scuba diving licence through University of California Santa Barbara (UCSB). This grew from a passion to something that I wanted to share with others which drove me to search for furthering this experience in diving up in Monterey Bay where I got my AAUS science diving certification. Ultimately I hope to share my love for the ocean with others and influence the next generation to value more sustainable practices as well as emphasise the importance of Science and knowledge.

title: Project_Overview
text: The dataset I plan to use in this project comes from the Partnership for Interdisciplinary Studies of Coastal Oceans (PISCO). PISCO collects long-term ecological monitoring data along the West Coast of North America; including fish abundance, species composition, and environmental conditions.

title: Project_Overview
text:
Data Visualization

Final Visualization Goals
The final visualizations will include:

Bar charts showing total or average fish counts by species
Line graphs showing trends in fish counts over time
Boxplots or violin charts comparing counts across regions



Missing or Needed Data Changes
To create these visualizations, the following changes may be needed:

Aggregating counts by species, site, or year
Handling missing or zero counts appropriately
Possibly grouping duplicate species into the same categories

title: Project_Overview
text:
Data Cleaning

Cleaning Steps

Reformat variables? The year variable may need to be converted into a date format, and species names may be converted to factors.
Handle missing data? Missing values in count or site variables will be addressed by removing incomplete rows or replacing with appropriate values.
Filter data? The dataset may be filtered to focus on specific regions, years, or species of interest.
Create new variables? New variables such as total counts per site/year or grouped species categories will be created.
Join additional data? Additional PISCO datasets may be joined for this analysis.
Summarize variables? Aggregated summaries (e.g., mean or total counts by species, site, or year) will be calculated.
